attacking_style: Participates in planned breakaways rather than late-race attacks. Appears in multi-rider moves with team support, though struggles to maintain pace when racing intensifies.
team_tactics: Cofidis deploys multiple riders to breakaways (2+ riders committed), following standard French team pattern of numerical presence in early moves rather than late positioning for leaders.
terrain_preference: Best suited to flatter breakaway stages where sustained tempo matters more than explosive climbing. Exposed on steep climbs like Roche-aux-Faucons where pure climbing power required.
vulnerability: Cannot sustain pace when break collaborations intensify - dropped 10-12 seconds from partner during Vuelta break. Lacks ability to hold position when caught on major climbs.
key_patterns: Deployed in multi-rider breakaway formations with teammates - pattern across both races observed
Struggles to maintain contact with break companions when pace increases under pressure
Cofidis uses him in standard French tactics: early numerical breakaway presence, not late positioning
form_trajectory: negative: Most recent observation (2018 Vuelta) showed inability to match planned break partner's pace
